summary: ANDROID | Falcon Grey | EQ settings not saved for Custom
scenario: Custom EQ settings are not persisted after saving and reopening the EQ settings page on Poly Studio Mobile app
description_clean: Custom EQ settings are not saved after clicking Save and reopening the EQ settings page. Steps: Connect a Poly device, go to EQ settings, make changes and click Save, then reopen EQ settings to verify. Observed: Settings are not saved. Expected: Settings are saved.
steps: 1. Launch the Poly Studio Mobile app on your Android device.
2. Ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected (e.g., Poly Voyager Focus 2).
3. From the Home screen, tap on the connected [Device Name] to open Device Settings.
5. Make custom adjustments to the EQ bands (e.g., boost bass, reduce treble).
6. Tap the Save button to save the custom EQ settings.
7. Exit the Equalizer screen by tapping the back arrow or navigating away.
8. Reopen the Equalizer by going back to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
9. Observe the EQ band positions and the active preset.
expected: The custom EQ settings (band adjustments) are retained and displayed as previously saved. The custom preset is shown as active.
actual: The custom EQ settings are not retained. The EQ bands reset to default/flat positions, and the custom preset is no longer active.
